items.headline: Food Delivery Fees Hit New York Residents Hard
items.summary: The city's food delivery industry has faced intense backlash over exorbitant fees, sparking calls for federal regulation. Local businesses are now feeling the pinch, affecting their bottom lines.

items.headline: Tree Care Services Expand to NYC
items.summary: AAA Tree Service NY Corp is expanding its services to New York City residents, offering comprehensive tree care solutions, from pruning to removal. The move aims to cater to the growing demand for urban tree maintenance.

items.headline: Indian Women Entrepreneurs Shine at NYC Exhibition
items.summary: A group of women entrepreneurs from Jalgaon has made a name for themselves in the city's exhibition circuit, showcasing their unique handicrafts and empowering local communities. Their journey from village roots to global recognition is a testament to the power of female entrepreneurship.

items.tip: Get there early, as the lines can get quite long. Try it with a side of knish or matzo ball soup for a classic New York experience. Katz's has been a Lower East Side staple since 1888.

items.dish: Black and White Cookie · Classic Checkerboard Cookie
items.tip: Levain's cookies are famous for being thick and gooey, so be prepared for a serious cookie experience. Try it with a cold glass of milk for the ultimate comfort food treat.

items.dish: Classic Cheeseburger · Juicy Patty, Melted Cheese, Crisp Lettuce
items.tip: Pete's has been serving up classic American comfort food since 1864. Order the cheeseburger with a side of fries and a cold beer for a quintessential New York experience.

items.fact: New York City is the most linguistically diverse city in the world, with as many as 800 languages spoken within its borders.
items.source: New York City Department of Education

items.fact: The Stonewall Inn in Greenwich Village is a designated U.S. National Historic Landmark and National Monument, marking the site of the June 1969 Stonewall riots and the birthplace of the modern gay rights movement.

New York City gave rise to numerous cultural movements, including the Harlem Renaissance in literature and visual art, abstract expressionism (also known as the New York School) in painting, and hip hop, punk, salsa, freestyle, Tin Pan Alley, and certain forms of jazz in music.
